Requirement: Runtime factory shall return an EncodingContext paired with the selected edition, provider, and transport.
EncodingRegistry shall resolve codecs by HLA encoding name and expose deterministic capability reports.
2010 and 2025 profiles shall include standard primitive encoding helper names.
FOM type resolver shall parse basic, simple, enumerated, reference, array, fixed record, and variant record data types.
Basic data resolver shall honor size, endian, and encoding metadata for custom basic representations.
Simple and enumerated data types shall delegate to their representation codec; enumerated names shall map to encoded numeric values.
Fixed record codec shall preserve FOM field order and reject missing, unexpected, or incompatible fields.
Array codec shall enforce fixed cardinality and support dynamic cardinality for variable arrays.
Variant record codec shall encode/decode discriminant and selected alternative, including HLAother fallback when declared.
Strict decode shall reject truncation, malformed data, trailing bytes, unknown discriminants, and unknown encoding names.
Provider-specific codecs may register custom names without mutating built-in codec behavior.
Provider oracle tests shall compare composite bytes against an available RTI EncoderFactory when configured.
Encoding evidence files shall be emitted for capabilities, type resolution, round trips, and negative tests.
